g.data-narration: Secure Shell (SSH) is a cryptographic network protocol providing secure remote access over unsecured networks.
SSH replaced insecure protocols like Telnet that transmitted passwords in plain text, vulnerable to interception.
SSH uses client-server architecture with three protocol layers: Transport, Authentication, and Connection.
The three SSH protocol layers handle encryption, authentication, and channel multiplexing respectively.
SSH2 is the current standard with improved security, Perfect Forward Secrecy, and fixes for SSH1 vulnerabilities.
SSH uses symmetric encryption for data, asymmetric for key exchange, and hashing for integrity verification.
SSH supports password, public key, keyboard-interactive, and host-based authentication methods.
g.text: Public Key authentication is the most secure and recommended method

g.data-narration: Three SSH key types: RSA (widely supported, 4096-bit recommended), ECDSA (elliptic curve), and Ed25519 (modern, recommended).
SSH key files are stored in ~/.ssh directory with strict permissions: 600 for private keys, 700 for the directory.
Local port forwarding (-L) forwards a local port through SSH server to a remote destination.
Remote port forwarding (-R) allows remote hosts to access local services through the SSH server.
Dynamic port forwarding (-D) creates a SOCKS proxy, allowing flexible forwarding through the SSH server.
g.text: ssh -D 1080 user@remote.com (Configure browser to use SOCKS proxy)

g.data-narration: SCP provides simple file copying, while SFTP offers interactive file operations with resume support.
SSH configuration files: ssh_config for client settings, sshd_config for server configuration.
Example: Generate Ed25519 key pair using ssh-keygen, copy public key to server for passwordless login.
g.text: Creates ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on server for passwordless login

g.data-narration: Example: Create SSH tunnel to access remote service locally using local port forwarding command.
g.text: Example: SSH Tunnel for Web App
Access local web app at http://localhost:9090 (forwards to remote localhost:8080)

g.data-narration: Example: Hardening SSH server with key authentication, custom port, disabled root login, and strong ciphers.
g.text: Apply with: sudo systemctl restart sshd

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: SSH provides secure remote access, uses layered architecture, supports multiple auth methods, and is essential for sysadmins.
